title: Cabo de Rama
tagline: Cabo de Rama — heritage city of Goa

overview.short: Praça do Cabo de Rama, also known as Cabo de Rama Fort, is a medieval hillfort located in Khola village, on the coast of Canacona, Goa in India.
overview.description: Praça do Cabo de Rama, also known as Cabo de Rama Fort, is a medieval hillfort located in Khola village, on the coast of Canacona, Goa in India.

topPlaces.name: Khola, Goa
topPlaces.description: Khola is a village located in Canacona, Goa, India. "Cola" is the variation of the same name.

topPlaces.name: Betul, Goa
topPlaces.description: Betul is a coastal village located in the Quepem taluka, South Goa district, Goa, India. It is the southernmost village of Quepem and is well-known for its beach, lighthouse, and fort.

topPlaces.name: Baradi, Goa
topPlaces.description: Baradi (Konkani: Barade) is a scenic neighborhood located within the village of Velim, Goa, neighbouring Tollecanto and Betul. It is situated in the Salcete taluka and falls under the South Goa district, in the Indian coastal state of Goa.

topPlaces.name: Mobor
topPlaces.description: Mobor is a town in South Goa in the state of Goa, India.

topPlaces.name: Cutbona
topPlaces.description: Cutbona (Konkani: Kutbonn) is a neighborhood located within the Velim village, neighbouring Betul. It is situated in the Salcete taluka and falls under the South Goa district, in the Indian coastal state of Goa.

topPlaces.name: Fatorpa
topPlaces.description: Fatorpa is a village in Quepem taluk, Kushavati district, Goa. There are two famous temples of Shree Shantadurga in this Village.

topPlaces.name: Tollecanto
topPlaces.description: Tollecanto is a neighborhood located within the village of Velim, situated in the Salcete taluka and falls under the South Goa district, in the Indian coastal state of Goa. It is about 17 km (11 mi) away from the Margao city and is also a part of the Velim Assembly constituency.

topPlaces.name: Cavelossim
topPlaces.description: Cavelossim is a village in South Goa district in the state of Goa, India. The village hosts a well-known beach at the southernmost tip of the Salcete beach stretch that starts from Majorda in the north to Cavelossim in the south.

items.note: Khola is a village located in Canacona, Goa, India. "Cola" is the variation of the same name.
Betul is a coastal village located in the Quepem taluka, South Goa district, Goa, India. It is the southernmost village of Quepem and is well-known for its beach, lighthouse, and fort.

Baradi (Konkani: Barade) is a scenic neighborhood located within the village of Velim, Goa, neighbouring Tollecanto and Betul. It is situated in the Salcete taluka and falls under the South Goa district, in the Indian coastal state of Goa.
Mobor is a town in South Goa in the state of Goa, India.

Cutbona (Konkani: Kutbonn) is a neighborhood located within the Velim village, neighbouring Betul. It is situated in the Salcete taluka and falls under the South Goa district, in the Indian coastal state of Goa.
Fatorpa is a village in Quepem taluk, Kushavati district, Goa. There are two famous temples of Shree Shantadurga in this Village.

Tollecanto is a neighborhood located within the village of Velim, situated in the Salcete taluka and falls under the South Goa district, in the Indian coastal state of Goa. It is about 17 km (11 mi) away from the Margao city and is also a part of the Velim Assembly constituency.

Cavelossim is a village in South Goa district in the state of Goa, India. The village hosts a well-known beach at the southernmost tip of the Salcete beach stretch that starts from Majorda in the north to Cavelossim in the south.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Cabo de Rama?
faq.a: The best time to visit Cabo de Rama is Nov – Feb. Summers are 26–36°C and winters are 22–32°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Cabo de Rama?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Goa International Airport, Dabolim (34 km) and the nearest railway station is Madgaon Junction (Goa) (21 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Cabo de Rama?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Cabo de Rama at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Cabo de Rama?
faq.a: Stays in Cabo de Rama start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Cabo de Rama?
faq.a: The top attractions are Khola, Goa, Betul, Goa, Baradi, Goa, Mobor, Cutbona.
